Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
Item Description: Identify obtuse angles in triangles.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
Item Description: Identify whether specified line segments and angles can be found in a given figure.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
Item Description: Identify the number of acute angles in a given figure.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
Item Description: Identify shapes that have a pair of parallel sides.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category, and identify right triangles.
Item Description: Identify shapes that have at least one obtuse angle and identify a right triangle.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category, and identify right triangles.
Item Description: Identify shapes that have at least one pair of parallel sides and at least one pair of perpendicular sides.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category, and identify right triangles.
Item Description: Determine which given shape is a quadrilateral with perpendicular sides.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
Item Description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure and identify how many lines of symmetry can be drawn on the figure.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
Item Description: Determine which shapes have at least one line of symmetry.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
Item Description: Identify the number of lines of symmetry in a given figure.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
Item Description: Identify a given shape that has a specified number of lines of symmetry.

Cluster: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
Standard Text: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
Item Description: Determine whether lines on geometric figures represent lines of symmetry.

Cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
Standard Text: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz.; l, ml; hr, min, sec. Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Record measurement equivalents in a two-column table. For example, know that 1 ft is 12 times as long as 1 in. Express the length of a 4 ft snake as 48 in. Generate a conversion table for feet and inches listing the number pairs (1, 12), (2, 24), (3, 36), …
Item Description: Convert dimensions measured in yards to feet.

Cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
Standard Text: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz.; l, ml; hr, min, sec. Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Record measurement equivalents in a two-column table. For example, know that 1 ft is 12 times as long as 1 in. Express the length of a 4 ft snake as 48 in. Generate a conversion table for feet and inches listing the number pairs (1, 12), (2, 24), (3, 36), …
Item Description: Convert from a larger unit of measure to a smaller unit of measure using minutes and seconds and order measurements given in yards, feet, and inches.

Cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
Standard Text: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz.; l, ml; hr, min, sec. Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Record measurement equivalents in a two-column table. For example, know that 1 ft is 12 times as long as 1 in. Express the length of a 4 ft snake as 48 in. Generate a conversion table for feet and inches listing the number pairs (1, 12), (2, 24), (3, 36), …
Item Description: Convert kilograms to grams.

Cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
Standard Text: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz.; l, ml; hr, min, sec. Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Record measurement equivalents in a two-column table. For example, know that 1 ft is 12 times as long as 1 in. Express the length of a 4 ft snake as 48 in. Generate a conversion table for feet and inches listing the number pairs (1, 12), (2, 24), (3, 36), …
Item Description: Identify a liquid measurement in liters from a diagram, convert liters to milliliters, convert hours and minutes to only minutes, and compare masses given in different metric units.

Cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
Standard Text: Use the four operations to solve word problems involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money, including problems involving simple fractions or decimals, and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Represent measurement quantities using diagrams such as number line diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
Item Description: Solve real-world problems involving reading an analog clock and adding and subtracting time intervals.

Cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
Standard Text:
Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real-world and mathematical problems.
For example, find the width of a rectangular room given the area of the flooring and the length, by viewing the area formula as a multiplication equation with an unknown factor. (Note: When finding areas of rectangular regions answers will be in square units. For example, the area of a 1 cm x 1 cm rectangular region will be 1 square centimeter (1 cm2, students are not expected to use this notation.) When finding the perimeter of a rectangular region answers will be in linear units. For example, the perimeter of the region is: 1cm + 1cm + 1cm +1cm = 4 cm or 2(1cm) + 2(1cm) = 4 cm).
Item Description: Given the length and the width of a rectangle, determine its area.

Cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
Standard Text:
Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real-world and mathematical problems.
For example, find the width of a rectangular room given the area of the flooring and the length, by viewing the area formula as a multiplication equation with an unknown factor. (Note: When finding areas of rectangular regions answers will be in square units. For example, the area of a 1 cm x 1 cm rectangular region will be 1 square centimeter (1 cm2, students are not expected to use this notation.) When finding the perimeter of a rectangular region answers will be in linear units. For example, the perimeter of the region is: 1cm + 1cm + 1cm +1cm = 4 cm or 2(1cm) + 2(1cm) = 4 cm).
Item Description: Use the area formula to find the area of a square.

Cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
Standard Text:
Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real-world and mathematical problems.
For example, find the width of a rectangular room given the area of the flooring and the length, by viewing the area formula as a multiplication equation with an unknown factor. Item Description: Determine the area of a rectangle given the length and width, determine the width of a rectangle given the area and length, explain how it is possible for two rectangles with different areas to have the same perimeter, and solve a real-world problem involving rectangles with the same perimeter but with different areas.

Item Description: Select the equation that shows how to find the perimeter of a rectangle given the length and width.

Cluster: Represent and interpret data.
Standard Text:
Make a line plot (dot plot) representation to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots (dot plots).
For example, from a line plot (dot plot) find and interpret the difference in length between the longest and shortest specimens in an insect collection.
Item Description: Solve a word problem with addition of fractions by using data from a dot plot.

Cluster: Represent and interpret data.
Standard Text:
Make a line plot (dot plot) representation to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots (dot plots).
For example, from a line plot (dot plot) find and interpret the difference in length between the longest and shortest specimens in an insect collection.
Item Description: Determine which line plot represents given data.

Cluster: Represent and interpret data.
Standard Text:
Make a line plot (dot plot) representation to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots (dot plots).
For example, from a line plot (dot plot) find and interpret the difference in length between the longest and shortest specimens in an insect collection.
Item Description: Solve a word problem with addition of whole numbers and fractions by using data from a dot plot.

Cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
Standard Text: An angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle. An angle that turns through 1/360 of a circle is called a “one-degree angle,” and can be used to measure angles.
Item Description: Determine the measure of an angle that turns through a given fraction of a circle.

Cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
Standard Text: An angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle. An angle that turns through 1/360 of a circle is called a “one-degree angle,” and can be used to measure angles.
Item Description: Give the measure of an angle that turns through a portion of a circle.

Cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
Standard Text: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
Item Description: Determine measures of given angles shown on protractors.

Cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
Standard Text: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
Item Description: Determine the measure of an angle shown with a drawing of a protractor.

Cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
Standard Text: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
Item Description: Determine measures of angles using a protractor.

Cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
Standard Text: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems, e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure.
Item Description: Determine an angle measure given the measures of two adjacent angles and the sum of all three angle measures.

Cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
Standard Text: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems, e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure.
Item Description: Determine an angle measure given the measure of an adjacent angle and the sum of both angle measures.

Cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
Standard Text: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems, e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure.
Item Description: Determine the angle measure of a larger angle given the measures of the individual angles that make up the larger angle.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text:
Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in any place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right. 
For example, recognize that 700 ÷ 70 = 10 by applying concepts of place value and division.
Item Description: In a given multi-digit number, recognize that the value of a digit is 10 times the value of the digit to its right.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text:
Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in any place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right. 
For example, recognize that 700 ÷ 70 = 10 by applying concepts of place value and division.
Item Description: Determine which number has a digit with a value that is 10 times the value of a digit in a given number.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form. Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.  [Note: Grade 4 expectations in this domain are limited to whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.]
Item Description: Write the standard form of a number given in word form.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form. Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.  [Note: Grade 4 expectations in this domain are limited to whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.]
Item Description: Express multi-digit whole numbers in standard and expanded forms and use place value understanding to compare the numbers with symbols and to explain your answer.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form. Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.  [Note: Grade 4 expectations in this domain are limited to whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.]
Item Description: Match numbers written in expanded form to their equivalent numbers written in word form, and compare numbers written in word form to a number in standard form.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
Item Description: Round multi-digit whole numbers to the nearest ten, hundred, and thousand.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
Item Description: Determine which multi-digit whole numbers, when rounded to the nearest ten thousand, round to a given number.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
Item Description: Round a multi-digit whole number to the nearest thousand, ten thousand, and hundred thousand.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
Item Description: Round a multi-digit whole number to the nearest hundred, thousand, and ten thousand.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
Item Description: Round a six-digit whole number to the nearest thousand.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
Item Description: Determine the difference of two five-digit numbers.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
Item Description: Given one three-digit addend and the three-digit sum, determine the missing addend.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
Item Description: Determine which set of student work given for a subtraction problem of a four-digit number minus a three-digit number is correct.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
Item Description: Determine the difference of a six-digit number and a four-digit number.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
Item Description: Subtract a four-digit whole number from a five-digit whole number.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
Item Description: Determine the product of a three-digit number and a one-digit number.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
Item Description: Solve word problems by multiplying whole numbers: two digits by one digit, two digits by two digits, and four digits by one digit.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
Item Description: Determine if the given products of a three-digit number and a one-digit number are correct.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
Item Description: Determine which statements showing related multiplication equations and division equations including a variable are correct.

Reporting Category: Number and Operations in Base Ten
Cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
Standard Text: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
Item Description: Determine the whole number quotient of a four-digit dividend and a one-digit divisor.

Cluster: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n x a)/(n x b) by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the numbers and sizes of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions, including fractions greater than 1.
Item Description: Identify a fraction model that represents a fraction equivalent to a given mixed number.

Cluster: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n x a)/(n x b) by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the numbers and sizes of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions, including fractions greater than 1.
Item Description: Determine the denominator that will make a fraction equivalent to a given fraction.

Cluster: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators, e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as 1/2. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
Item Description: Identify the correct comparisons of two fractions that are represented by visual fraction models.

Cluster: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators, e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as 1/2. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
Item Description: Determine which number sentences with the symbols <, >, or = correctly compare two given fractions with different numerators and denominators.

Cluster: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators, e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as 1/2. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
Item Description: Write a fraction comparison using symbols, compare fractions with different denominators, and critique the reasoning of others about different-sized wholes in a word problem.

Cluster: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators, e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as 1/2. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
Item Description: Identify a fraction that will make a comparison statement with another fraction true.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Decompose a fraction into a sum of fractions with the same denominator in more than one way, recording each decomposition by an equation. Justify decompositions, e.g., by using drawings or visual fraction models. Examples: 3/8 = 1/8 + 1/8 + 1/8 ; 3/8 = 1/8 + 2/8 ; 2 1/8 = 1 + 1 + 1/8 = 8/8 + 8/8 + 1/8.
Item Description: Determine which expression has a value that is not equivalent to a given expression.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: a. Understand addition and subtraction of fractions as joining and separating parts referring to the same whole. (The whole can be a set of objects.)
Item Description: Determine which fraction model represents the sum of two given fractions with like denominators.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators, e.g., by using drawings or visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
Item Description: Determine which addition equation involving fractions represents a given real-world context.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators, e.g., by using drawings or visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
Item Description: Determine which model represents the answer to a real-world problem involving addition and subtraction of fractions with like denominators.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Understand a fraction a/b with a > 1 as a sum of fractions 1/b.
Item Description: Solve word problems by identifying an equation that shows the sum of two fractions, justifying if a given equation is correct or not, adding and subtracting fractions, and subtracting mixed numbers.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem. For example, if each person at a party will eat 3/8 of a pound of roast beef, and there will be 5 people at the party, how many pounds of roast beef will be needed? Between what two whole numbers does your answer lie?
Item Description: Solve a word problem by multiplying a fraction by a whole number.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem. For example, if each person at a party will eat 3/8 of a pound of roast beef, and there will be 5 people at the party, how many pounds of roast beef will be needed? Between what two whole numbers does your answer lie?
Item Description: Solve a word problem by multiplying a fraction by a whole number.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem. For example, if each person at a party will eat 3/8 of a pound of roast beef, and there will be 5 people at the party, how many pounds of roast beef will be needed? Between what two whole numbers does your answer lie?
Item Description: Solve a word problem by multiplying a fraction by a whole number.

Cluster: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers for fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
Standard Text: Understand a fraction a/b as a multiple of 1/b. For example, use a visual fraction model to represent 5/4 as the product 5 x (1/4), recording the conclusion by the equation 5/4 = 5 x (1/4).
Item Description: Determine the fraction model that represents the product of a whole number and a unit fraction.

Cluster: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
Standard Text: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100, and use this technique to add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100. For example, express 3/10 as 30/100, and add 3/10 + 4/100 = 34/100.
Item Description: Add fractions with denominators of 10 and 100.

Cluster: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
Standard Text: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100, and use this technique to add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100. For example, express 3/10 as 30/100, and add 3/10 + 4/100 = 34/100.
Item Description: Determine the numerator of a fraction with a denominator of 100 that makes it equivalent to a given fraction with a denominator of 10.

Cluster: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
Standard Text: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100, and use this technique to add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100. For example, express 3/10 as 30/100, and add 3/10 + 4/100 = 34/100.
Item Description: Identify two fractions with denominators of 10 and 100 that are equivalent.

Cluster: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
Standard Text:
Use decimal notation to represent fractions with denominators 10 or 100. 
For example, rewrite 0.62 as 62/100; describe a length as 0.62 meters; locate 0.62 on a number line diagram.
Item Description: Determine the location of a given decimal on a number line.

Cluster: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
Standard Text: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two decimals refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual model.
Item Description: Compare decimals given in tenths and hundredths in a list, order decimals from least to greatest, and determine which decimal from the list is closest in value to a given decimal.

Item Description: Identify correct comparison statements of two decimals to hundredths and tenths using the symbols > or <.

Cluster: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.
Standard Text: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison, e.g., interpret 35 = 5 x 7 as a statement that 35 is 5 times as many as 7 and 7 times as many as 5. Represent verbal statements of multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations.
Item Description: Determine which equation represents a multiplicative comparison in a word problem.

Cluster: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.

Item Description: Determine which multiplication equation represents a given word comparison and which word comparison represents a given multiplication equation.

Cluster: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.
Standard Text: Multiply or divide to solve word problems involving multiplicative comparison, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem, distinguishing multiplicative comparison from additive comparison. [Note: See Glossary, Table 2.]
Item Description: Write an equation with a symbol for the unknown number to represent a word problem involving multiplicative comparison and then multiply to solve problems.

Cluster: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.
Standard Text: Solve multistep word problems posed with whole numbers and having whole-number answers using the four operations, including problems in which remainders must be interpreted. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding.
Item Description: Solve a multi-step word problem involving addition and division of whole numbers.

Item Description: Given a multi-step word problem, divide whole numbers and interpret the remainder to determine which statements that describe the problem are true.

Item Description: Solve a multi-step real-world problem using multiplication and division, and by interpreting a remainder.

Cluster: Gain familiarity with factors and multiples.
Standard Text: Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the range 1–100. Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors. Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1–100 is a multiple of a given one-digit number. Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1–100 is prime or composite.
Item Description: Given a set of multiples of a number, determine the number.

Cluster: Generate and analyze patterns.
Standard Text: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule. Identify apparent features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself. For example, given the rule “Add 3” and the starting number 1, generate terms in the resulting sequence and observe that the terms appear to alternate between odd and even numbers. Explain informally why the numbers will continue to alternate in this way.
Item Description: Determine a specified term in a pattern given the first number and the rule of the pattern.

Explain informally why the numbers will continue to alternate in this way.
Item Description: Determine the next step in a shape pattern, identify how many shapes will be in future steps, and demonstrate understanding of the relationships between different features of the pattern.
